title: Nerine humilis
taxonomy: Nerine humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, specifically within the genus Nerine. The genus Nerine is further divided into several species, with N. humilis being one of them. N. humilis is native to South Africa, where it grows in rocky outcrops and dry areas. The classification of N. humilis is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf shape, flower color, and growth habit. This classification helps botanists and horticulturists to identify and distinguish N. humilis from other similar species.

intro: Nerine humilis, also known as the Cape lily, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Nerine humilis include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, such as nerine, nerinine, and galanthamine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Nerine humilis is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, coughs, and sore throats.
